method: See README.md. SQS = deterministic proxy. rubric_verdict = dev-skill discipline RUBRIC applied by an independent reviewer agent (Sonnet, read-only). fqd = functional baseline-differential pass-rate delta (added for the 3-skill anchor).

results.rubric_note: Accurate, actionable, internally consistent guidance in an under-documented domain; two bounded gaps. Pre-retune SQS (48.3, distillation) under-rated it; guidance discipline lifts it to 80.1, agreeing with rubric SHIP and the +50% functional lift.

Strong before/after pattern coverage, but Pattern 18 (curly quotes) unusable as written; tension between 'add mess' and 'strip structure'. Guidance reclassification fixes the structural under-rating; rubric still flags a content issue (which SQS cannot see).

Sound, valuable 6-step framework; rubric flags over-broad trigger + unstated process/output relationship (content). Largest functional lift in the anchor (+75%) despite the lowest pre-retune SQS — the headline divergence.

Reads as a prose framework; rubric judges it SHOULD be a tagged rule pack (no references/, no impact ratings, below rule-count minimum). Guidance discipline is too generous here — a case where SQS measures prose structure but the rubric correctly flags that the skill ought to be RESTRUCTURED. Disambiguated by rubric, not SQS.
Structurally clean and accurate, but Recipe 4 heading misnames acquireRelease vs acquireUseRelease — a factual API error in a CRITICAL file.
Excellent scripts/workflow, but update-weights.sh mutates config.json with no dry-run/backup; line-proximity resolution heuristic can corrupt weights.
Strong pipeline & guardrails, but &&/|| precedence bug in detectors.sh mis-detects Justfile (capital) — corrupts the primary happy path.
Thorough pre-flight guardrails, but scan.sh feeds plain text to jq --slurpfile -> hard crash on any manual-debounce candidate; AGENTS.md is a stub.
Templates render valid React 19, conventions well-documented; but undocumented {type_imports_block} placeholder and a missing client-island.test.tsx.template.
Claims accurate, code production-ready, 43 rules well-balanced; only a mildly overstated bundle-size headline (self-corrected inline).

Excellent quantified rules and detection guidance, but useDeferredValue example contradicts its own pairing table; rules 2.3/4.5 give conflicting resolutions. Highest SQS in the anchor yet ZERO functional lift — a strong baseline model already writes idiomatic React 19, so the (well-built) skill adds no marginal value on this task.
42 balanced rules, high-authority references, production-realistic code; minor impact-quantification gaps on 2 sampled rules.
